name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow is the leading enterprise AI platform, orchestrating 80 billion+ enterprise workflows annually across IT, HR, customer service, security, and finance. The company's Now Platform connects systems, data, and processes with AI to eliminate friction in enterprise operations. ServiceNow serves over 8,100 customers including 85% of the Fortune 500. Q1 2026 subscription revenue was $3.67B (up 22% YoY), with FY2026 subscription revenue guidance raised to $15.74-15.78B.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): ServiceNow has fundamentally transformed from an ITSM ticketing system to an enterprise AI platform — a successful business model pivot that expanded TAM from ~$20B to $220B+ over the past decade [1]. The platform now orchestrates AI agents across enterprise workflows, with 350+ net new GenAI innovations shipped in the Kokai platform release and Now Assist becoming the fastest-selling new product in company history [2]. AI Deployment (2/2): AI agents are shipping to customers with measurable adoption; Q1 2026 cRPO grew 22.5% YoY to $12.64B, directly attributable to AI workflow expansion, with 16 transactions over $5M in net new ACV representing nearly 80% YoY growth [1]. AI is generating quantifiable revenue acceleration. Market Share (2/2): ServiceNow is the category-defining leader in enterprise workflow orchestration with no close independent competitor; 85% of Fortune 500 are customers, and net retention rate exceeds 125% [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): Armis cybersecurity acquisition expected to close H2 2026, Moveworks AI integration underway, and agentic AI features in development, but specific next-gen product launch timelines beyond current platform are limited. R&D Intensity (1/1): R&D spending is above industry average for enterprise software, supporting continuous platform innovation across IT, HR, security, and customer service verticals [1].

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): ServiceNow's purpose — 'make the world of work, work better for people' — is specific, thoughtful, and connected to genuine societal benefit of removing friction from enterprise workflows to free humans for higher-value work [2]. The mission is clearly lived through product decisions (workflow automation that empowers rather than replaces workers) and has been consistent under McDermott's leadership since 2019. Community Impact (1/2): ServiceNow.org provides platform access to nonprofits and the company has committed to carbon neutrality, demonstrating community investment beyond core business [3]. Employee Retention (1/2): ServiceNow is consistently recognized as a Glassdoor Best Place to Work with CEO McDermott earning 90%+ approval ratings; Fortune World's Most Admired Companies recognition and the 'WE' culture with 29,187 employees suggest above-average retention [2]. Insider Buying (2/2): CEO Bill McDermott purchased $3M of NOW shares in open-market buying during the March 2026 software sector rout and extended his contract to 2030, demonstrating strong personal conviction with meaningful financial commitment [2].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored as a growth stock with near-perfect metrics. Q1 2026 subscription revenue of $3.67B grew 22% YoY (19% constant currency), with FY2026 guidance raised to $15.74-15.78B representing 20.5-21% growth [1]. D/E of 20.7% is well below 70% target (scores 1.0). ROE of 16.1% exceeds 15% target (scores 1.0, 2x weight). Revenue growth YoY of 22.1% exceeds 10% target (scores 1.0). 3-year revenue CAGR of 22.4% vastly exceeds 10% target (scores 1.0, 2x weight). Forward P/E of 18.0x is below the 20x target (scores 1.0). Profit margin of 12.6% scores 0.63 (target 20%). Forward PEG of 0.88 is below the 1.0 target (scores 1.0, 2x weight). Weighted sum: 9.63/10 = 9.6, rounds to 10. The stock split has brought the forward P/E to a remarkably attractive 18x for a 22% growth company [4].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): ServiceNow's core thesis is that AI creates more demand for enterprise workflow orchestration, not less. CEO McDermott argues that 'AI excels at identifying problems and providing advice but cannot execute solutions' — and ServiceNow controls the execution layer [2]. The company orchestrates 80B+ enterprise workflows annually, and AI agents require orchestration infrastructure to function reliably across enterprise systems. JPMorgan has explicitly flagged ServiceNow as 'AI-resilient' software. Regulatory compliance, audit trails, and integration with legacy enterprise environments create switching costs that pure AI cannot replicate. However, the risk that agentic AI matures to replace workflow orchestration platforms within 2-3 years, though low probability, is non-zero. AI Opportunity (1/1): Now Assist is ServiceNow's fastest-selling new product ever, generating quantifiable AI-specific ACV; 16 deals over $5M in Q1 2026 (80% YoY growth) are directly attributable to AI workflow capabilities [1].

infinite_game.summary: Bill McDermott is a rare enterprise software CEO who operates explicitly with long-term platform thinking. His 'McDermott Playbook' — organic innovation over dilutive acquisitions, Rule of 55+ profitability standard (revenue growth + FCF margin must exceed 55), contract extension to 2030, and $3M personal share purchase during a sector rout — reflects genuine infinite game commitment. ServiceNow's Just Cause of removing workplace friction is validated by consistent platform expansion, 22% subscription growth, and sustained >125% net retention rates across economic cycles.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): ServiceNow's cause — making work work better for people by eliminating enterprise friction — is genuine, service-oriented, and specific [2]. It describes a future state (frictionless enterprise workflows) that doesn't fully exist yet and invites technology partners and customers to contribute. McDermott's consistent articulation across economic cycles and the company's product decisions (AI that empowers workers rather than replaces them) validate the cause through execution. Not yet tested through transformative sacrifice at the Sinek canonical level. Display score: 7.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (2/2): ServiceNow's 'WE' culture uniting 29,187 employees under the DESCO21C vision, Glassdoor Best Place to Work recognition, CEO approval ratings above 90%, and Fortune World's Most Admired Companies designation demonstrate a high-trust organization [2]. McDermott's 'people before profits' leadership philosophy and the human-AI narrative — framing AI as amplifying employees rather than replacing them — creates genuine psychological safety. Low voluntary attrition relative to tech sector peers. Display score: 10.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): McDermott publicly acknowledges Salesforce, SAP, and Microsoft as worthy rivals whose platform capabilities push ServiceNow to develop better integrations and expand its own platform [2]. The Moveworks and Armis acquisitions reflect competitive awareness — rivals' strengths revealing portfolio gaps that ServiceNow addresses through strategic action rather than dismissal. Display score: 10.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): The pivot from ITSM ticketing (2004-2015) to enterprise platform (2015-2020) to enterprise AI platform (2020-present) represents textbook existential flexibility — each transformation built on the previous while expanding TAM 10x from $20B to $220B+ [1]. The company didn't defend its ITSM monopoly; it redefined the category. Now Assist and agentic AI represent the latest reinvention, not the first. Display score: 10.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): McDermott's $3M personal share purchase during the January-March 2026 software sector rout — when the stock dropped 14% on AI disruption fears — demonstrates acting in service of the Just Cause at personal financial cost [1][2]. The Rule of 55+ standard maintains long-term capital discipline even when it constrains short-term growth. However, these decisions are made from a position of strength (market-leading platform, 22% growth) rather than genuine sacrifice against headwinds. Display score: 6.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow is the enterprise AI platform that benefits from — rather than is threatened by — the AI boom. Q1 2026 subscription revenue of $3.67B grew 22% YoY (accelerating from 19.5% prior quarter), with cRPO of $12.64B growing 22.5% and 16 deals over $5M in net new ACV representing 80% YoY growth [1]. FY2026 subscription guidance raised to $15.74-15.78B (~21% growth) with expanding operating margins. At 18x forward earnings and 0.88 PEG following the stock split, ServiceNow offers rare value for a 22% growth enterprise software leader. CEO McDermott's $3M personal share purchase and 2030 contract extension signal maximum long-term conviction [2]. Now Assist is the fastest-selling new product in company history, validating AI as revenue additive.
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on geopolitical and execution risk. Q1 2026 saw approximately 75 basis points of subscription revenue headwind from delayed Middle East on-premise deal closings due to the Iran conflict, and the stock sank 14% post-earnings despite strong results [1]. If agentic AI matures faster than McDermott's 'execution layer' thesis anticipates, enterprise workflows could be replaced rather than orchestrated by AI systems — a low-probability but high-impact disruption scenario. Profit margin of 12.6% on a GAAP basis remains below the 20% target, reflecting heavy stock-based compensation that dilutes reported profitability. The Armis acquisition (expected H2 2026 close) adds integration complexity and potential execution risk in a high-multiple stock where any stumble triggers outsized sell-offs.
key_catalysts: FY2026 subscription revenue guidance raise beyond $15.78B at Q2 report, demonstrating sustained 22%+ growth acceleration driven by AI workflow adoption and Now Assist ACV momentum
Armis cybersecurity acquisition closing in H2 2026, expanding platform TAM to security operations and adding incremental revenue to the Rule of 55+ framework
AI agent adoption metrics materializing with specific customer workflow automation data showing Now Assist driving measurable enterprise productivity gains and contract expansions
key_risks: Agentic AI systems maturing to replace enterprise workflow orchestration platforms within 2-3 years, faster than McDermott's 'execution layer' thesis anticipates — a structural disruption risk that would invalidate the platform's value proposition
Geopolitical disruption: Q1 2026 already showed 75bps subscription headwind from Middle East conflict; escalation could delay further large enterprise deals and compress growth below 20%

GAAP profit margin of 12.6% reflects heavy stock-based compensation; if SBC grows faster than revenue, the gap between non-GAAP (32% operating margin) and GAAP profitability will widen, pressuring the stock on an economic earnings basis
sources: ServiceNow Q1 2026 Financial Results Press Release — $3.67B subscription revenue, 22% YoY growth, guidance raised, April 22, 2026
Bill McDermott Leadership Profile, AI Strategy, $3M Share Purchase, Glassdoor/Fortune Recognition — ServiceNow.com, Semafor, BBN Times, LEADERS Magazine, 2025-2026
ServiceNow ESG and Community Commitments — ServiceNow.com, 2025-2026
